ogTitle: Iguana Creek vs Illawarra: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Iguana Creek and Illawarra.
heading: Iguana Creek vs Illawarra — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Iguana Creek, Victoria and Illawarra, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Iguana Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Illawarra (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Iguana Creek or Illawarra safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Iguana Creek has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Illawarra scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Iguana Creek scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Illawarra scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Iguana Creek or Illawarra?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Iguana Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
